note_text: Started taking metformin 500mg twice daily for my blood sugar. Refilled at the pharmacy this morning.

expect.rationale: Owner-authored first-party medication is a health fact; not cross-subject, commits.

note_text: Dr. Reyes confirmed today that I have type 2 diabetes. We'll manage it with diet first.

expect.rationale: Owner's own confirmed diagnosis is an asserted health state; commits under health.

note_text: Fasting glucose came back at 142 mg/dL this morning. Higher than last time.

expect.rationale: Lab reading is a measurement (value+unit) under health; measurements accumulate, never supersede.

note_text: Had my gallbladder removed last Tuesday. Recovery is going fine.

expect.rationale: A completed procedure is a past health event; events accumulate and commit under health.

note_text: Booked a follow-up with Dr. Patel for next Thursday at 2pm to review my thyroid.

expect.rationale: A future appointment with a clinician is an expected health event; expected must not overwrite present truth, but the appointment fact itself commits under health.

note_text: Opened a high-yield savings account at Ally to park the emergency fund.

expect.rationale: Owner's bank account is a finance fact; commits under finance.

note_text: Checking balance is $3,420.18 after the rent payment cleared.

expect.rationale: Account balance is a measurement (amount+currency) under finance; accumulates as a time series.

note_text: Moved my mortgage servicing over to Wells Fargo this month.

expect.rationale: Financial institution relationship is a finance fact; functional (single servicer) so supersession applies, commits under finance.

note_text: Maxed out my Roth IRA contributions for the year at Vanguard.

expect.rationale: A Roth IRA fund held at an institution is a finance fact; commits under finance.

note_text: My new address is 482 Birch Lane, Portland, OR 97214.

expect.rationale: An address is a value string on the owner's address fact, NOT a place entity; commits under location.

note_text: We finally moved out of the old apartment on Elm Street into the house in Beaverton.

expect.rationale: A home move is a newest-wins state transition on the owner's address; the move supersedes the prior address, both under location.

note_text: Spent the weekend in Sedona hiking Cathedral Rock. Stunning trip.

expect.rationale: A place visited is a location event; Sedona is a real Place entity (unlike a bare address string); commits under location.

note_text: Talked to Mom on the phone. She says she takes lisinopril for her blood pressure now.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-mom' name 'Mom' (Person)

expect.rationale: Relayed claim -> Mom cross_subject=true; never an owner medication fact.

note_text: Got a text from my brother Marcus. He wrote: "I just got diagnosed with celiac, finally an answer."
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-marcus' name 'Marcus' (Person)

expect.rationale: Quoted 'I' attributes to the speaker (Marcus), cross_subject; never an owner diagnosis.

note_text: Honestly everyone in the house is diabetic, me included. It's a family thing.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-celine' name 'Celine' (Person)
- id 'ent-theo' name 'Theo' (Person)

expect.rationale: A vague collective claim must not auto-write diabetes onto named family members.

note_text: Took my wife Celine to the airport this morning before work.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-celine' name 'Celine' (Person)

expect.rationale: 'my wife Celine' is owner-authored first-party, NOT cross_subject.

note_text: Long day. Took my daughter Lily to her allergist who upped her EpiPen prescription. Then a brutal sprint review at work where we slipped the launch to August. Trying to keep up my morning runs through all this.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-lily' name 'Lily' (Person)

expect.rationale: One general-captured journal note carries per-fact domains: Lily's medication floors to health even from a general note (and is owner-observed about a dependent child, first-party not cross_subject); the work and habit facts stay general. Each fact gets its own domain.

note_text: Quick brain dump before bed. Need to email the landlord, pick up dry cleaning, and my blood pressure was 148/95 at the drugstore machine today which is too high.

expect.rationale: Domain floor: a health-predicate fact (blood pressure reading) in a general-captured note still gets domain=health, ratcheting up without review. The errands are not durable facts.
